contents.markdown:
# Video: 00:00.733 => 00:43.233
Width: 1080
Height: 608

Transcript
```
WEBVTT

00:01.360 --> 00:06.640
<Speaker 1>When it comes to the neural TTS, in order to get a good voice, it's better to have good data.

00:07.120 --> 00:13.320
<Speaker 2>To achieve that, we build a universal TTS model based on 3,000 hours of data.

00:13.440 --> 00:23.680
<Speaker 1>We actually accumulated tons of the data so that this universal model is able to capture the nuance of the audio and generate a more natural voice for the algorithm.

00:24.040 --> 00:29.120
<Speaker 3>What we liked about cognitive services offerings were that they had a much higher fidelity.

00:29.600 --> 00:32.880
<Speaker 3>And they sounded a lot more like an actual human voice.

00:33.680 --> 00:37.200
<Speaker 4>Orlando ground 9555 requesting the end of pushback.

00:38.680 --> 00:41.280
<Speaker 4>9555 request to end pushback received.
```

Summary.valueString: The video opens with a scenic aerial view of an island and a small plane flying, displaying the Microsoft Flight Simulator and Microsoft Azure AI logos. It then transitions to an interview setting where a man discusses the importance of high-quality data for neural text-to-speech (TTS) technology. He explains the development of a universal TTS model trained on 3,000 hours of data to capture audio nuances and produce natural-sounding voices. The segment highlights the superior fidelity of cognitive services' offerings, emphasizing their human-like sound quality. The audio ends with a simulated air traffic control communication between Orlando ground and a plane (flight 9555) requesting and receiving clearance to end pushback.

transcriptPhrases.speaker: Speaker 1
transcriptPhrases.text: When it comes to the neural TTS, in order to get a good voice, it's better to have good data.

transcriptPhrases.speaker: Speaker 2
transcriptPhrases.text: To achieve that, we build a universal TTS model based on 3,000 hours of data.

transcriptPhrases.speaker: Speaker 1
transcriptPhrases.text: We actually accumulated tons of the data so that this universal model is able to capture the nuance of the audio and generate a more natural voice for the algorithm.

transcriptPhrases.speaker: Speaker 3
transcriptPhrases.text: What we liked about cognitive services offerings were that they had a much higher fidelity.

transcriptPhrases.speaker: Speaker 3
transcriptPhrases.text: And they sounded a lot more like an actual human voice.

transcriptPhrases.speaker: Speaker 4
transcriptPhrases.text: Orlando ground 9555 requesting the end of pushback.

transcriptPhrases.speaker: Speaker 4
transcriptPhrases.text: 9555 request to end pushback received.

Summary.valueString: The video begins with a scenic aerial view of an island and a small plane flying, introducing the collaboration between Microsoft Flight Simulator and Microsoft Azure AI. It then transitions to an interview with a man discussing the importance of high-quality data for neural text-to-speech (TTS) technology, mentioning the creation of a universal TTS model trained on 3,000 hours of data to capture audio nuances and produce natural-sounding voices. Visuals include sound waveforms representing audio data and shots of a data center, emphasizing the technical infrastructure behind the AI. The interview continues with another man explaining the high fidelity and human-like quality of cognitive services' voice offerings. The segment concludes with realistic flight simulation footage showing planes taxiing and preparing for takeoff, highlighting the integration of AI in enhancing the simulation experience.

Summary.valueString: The video opens with a scenic aerial view of an island and a small plane flying, introducing the collaboration between Microsoft Flight Simulator and Microsoft Azure AI. It then transitions to an interview setting where a man discusses the importance of high-quality data for neural text-to-speech (TTS) technology, mentioning the creation of a universal TTS model trained on 3,000 hours of data to capture audio nuances and produce natural-sounding voices. The interview highlights the superior fidelity of cognitive services' TTS offerings, emphasizing their human-like sound quality. The segment concludes with a brief audio exchange simulating air traffic control communications.

transcriptPhrases.speaker: Speaker 1
transcriptPhrases.text: When it comes to the neural TTS, in order to get a good voice, it's better to have good data.

transcriptPhrases.speaker: Speaker 2
transcriptPhrases.text: To achieve that, we build a universal TTS model based on 3,000 hours of data.

transcriptPhrases.speaker: Speaker 1
transcriptPhrases.text: We actually accumulated tons of the data so that this universal model is able to capture the nuance of the audio and generate a more natural voice for the algorithm.

transcriptPhrases.speaker: Speaker 3
transcriptPhrases.text: What we liked about cognitive services offerings were that they had a much higher fidelity.

transcriptPhrases.speaker: Speaker 3
transcriptPhrases.text: And they sounded a lot more like an actual human voice.

transcriptPhrases.speaker: Speaker 4
transcriptPhrases.text: Orlando ground 9555 requesting the end of pushback.

transcriptPhrases.speaker: Speaker 4
transcriptPhrases.text: 9555 request to end pushback received.
